seoTitle: Leawood Gardens vs Leighton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Leawood Gardens and Leighton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Leawood Gardens vs Leighton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Leawood Gardens and Leighton.
heading: Leawood Gardens vs Leighton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Leawood Gardens, South Australia and Leighton, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Leawood Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Leighton (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Leawood Gardens or Leighton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Leawood Gardens has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Leighton scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Leawood Gardens scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Leighton scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Leawood Gardens or Leighton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Leawood Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
